d9f60c9f4daa.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
d9f60c9f4daa.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
d9f60c9f4daa.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

b6992a30e1e1.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
b6992a30e1e1.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

0d9cb5fbca25.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
0d9cb5fbca25.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
0d9cb5fbca25.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

11a404899964.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
11a404899964.description: Your 4.6/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P44.4). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
11a404899964.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

50525b5e2e03.title: Ranked #6 of 10 locally — peers are ahead
50525b5e2e03.description: At percentile 44.4, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

cbcdbef7a3aa.title: Protect the 4.6/5 rating — it took years to build
cbcdbef7a3aa.description: With 1157 reviews at 4.6/5, your discovery advantage is substantial. Protect it by responding to every review within 48 hours and prompting satisfied tables for feedback.
